strategic_objectives: preserve shipping lanes and oil stability
keep coalition access and basing intact
retain escalation dominance without committing to open-ended occupation

strengths: integrated air and missile defense partnerships
preferred_instruments: base hardening and air defense reinforcement
readiness_drivers: partner confidence in US staying power

decision_doctrine.escalation_bias: calibrated coercion backed by overwhelming surge capacity
decision_doctrine.negotiation_posture: prefers negotiation from a visible position of force
red_lines: sustained closure of the Strait of Hormuz
large strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure tied to Iran
direct attacks on Israel that threaten regime-level escalation
off_ramps: proxy pause verified across multiple fronts

economic_pressure_points: shipping disruption in Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb
insurance and freight price surges for Gulf traffic
perceived loss of deterrence that weakens allied investment confidence
intelligence_priorities: missile and drone salvos aimed at US basing
cross-theater coupling between Gulf, Levant, and Red Sea fronts

priority_fronts.name: Central Gulf
priority_fronts.goal: keep Hormuz open and preserve base survivability
trigger_indicators: missile or drone launches toward Gulf facilities

priority_fronts.name: Levant Support Arc
priority_fronts.goal: backstop Israel while limiting uncontrolled regional spillover

priority_fronts.name: Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb
priority_fronts.goal: contain secondary shipping shocks and escort burden
trigger_indicators: missile or drone threats to transiting vessels

priority_fronts.name: Iraq-Syria Transit Corridor
priority_fronts.goal: watch militia movement and protect distributed personnel
trigger_indicators: new militia messaging from Tehran-aligned groups

priority_fronts.name: Domestic Political Front
priority_fronts.goal: avoid a perception of drift, overreach, or oil-price incompetence
trigger_indicators: market selloff tied to war risk

strategic_relationships.notes: Support is politically resilient but strain rises under prolonged high-volume conflict.

strategic_relationships.dependency: energy stability and Gulf basing environment
strategic_relationships.notes: Useful for market continuity and regional signaling, but cautious about direct war ownership.

strategic_relationships.dependency: logistics, port access, and air defense cooperation
strategic_relationships.notes: High infrastructure value makes force protection central.
Base access and mediation role make disruption politically costly.
Quiet cooperation is valuable and politically sensitive.

strategic_relationships.dependency: mutual signaling to avoid accidental regional runaway
strategic_relationships.notes: US reads Iran through proxy behavior, shipping activity, and missile posture.

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Bab el-Mandeb
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: commercially usable without continuous crisis premiums

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Eastern Mediterranean air-sea corridor
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: available for reinforcement and deterrent positioning

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Iraq-Jordan transit arc
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: stable enough for sustainment and evacuation pathways

geospatial_focus: Northern Israel and eastern Mediterranean support arc
